metadata.description: A managed relational database instance. Publishes connection outputs other resources bind to. Reconciled 2026-07-07 with the dcm-project catalog DatabaseSpec (#271): resources now required (matching their DatabaseSpec), and the storage/host relationships added so a database's data volume and placement are first-class edges.

version.description: Engine version. Concrete (e.g. `16`, engine-dependent — see registry conditional-constraints, E3) OR an abstract channel the PROVIDER resolves (`latest`, `lts`). Optional: when omitted it defaults to `latest`. Because the value may be abstract, two provider obligations apply (ADR-014; provider-contract §8.1a): (1) the provider DECLARES its channel→version resolution so an abstract request stays comparable/conformable/validatable against a pinned requirement and across compatible providers (declared, not live-queried); (2) the concrete version actually provisioned is recorded in outputs.applied_version for audit. Intent carries the abstract; realized carries the concrete.

instance_size.description: Provider-neutral size class (e.g. small|medium|large, or a named profile). Generic sizing INTENT — usable in place of, or alongside, explicit cpu/memory/storage. The PROVIDER reconciles it to concrete resources / its own instance classes at naturalization (DCM ADR-023, provider-naturalization boundary); UDLM/DCM does NOT define the mapping. A shared sizing option available to any resource type (common-elements §sizing).

cpu.description: CPU allocation, as a millicore string (`500m`) or whole/fractional cores (`2`, `1.5`) — Kubernetes-aligned quantity convention (#40 review). The provider reconciles to its concrete units at naturalization.

applied_version.description: The concrete engine version the provider actually provisioned (audit record; realized-side). When the requested `version` was abstract (`latest`/`lts`) or omitted, this captures what it resolved to at realization — closing the loop between abstract intent and reality. Populated by the provider on realization (intent→realized; provider-contract §8.1a obligation 3).

relationships.name: data_volume
relationships.description: The persistent volume backing the database's data directory (binds its volume_handle output). Ordering: the volume is realized before the database that stores on it.

relationships.name: host
relationships.description: The compute host/guest the database runs on, when self-hosted (vs a managed DBaaS provider).

relationships.name: cluster
relationships.description: The cluster the database runs on, when containerized/operator-managed.
